consts:
Build seg_targets.bin via single-class peel split + 4-class AC residual.

Direction 1 (c2 variant) from the parallel-research synthesis 2026-04-11.
Sister module to encode_seg_v3_purepy.py — same range coder, same Markov-3
spatial / Markov-4 temporal context structure, but the dominant peel class
(default c2 = sky/background, 49.5% of pixels) is pulled out into a
separately compressed binary mask. The arithmetic-coded residual then has
a 4-symbol alphabet over the 50.5% of pixels that aren't c2.

Crucial detail: the residual encoder uses the FULL 5-class context (top,
left, top-left, prev) — i.e. the model knows when a neighbour was the peel
class — even though it only ever EMITS one of the 4 non-peel symbols.
That makes it strictly tighter than the c2_entropy.py Strategy B estimate
which collapsed contexts to 4 classes.

On-disk format ("seg_targets.bin"):

    uint16 n_pairs, uint16 H, uint16 W
    uint8  precision         # typically 16
    uint8  peel_class        # 0..N_CLASSES-1
    uint8  mask_format       # 0=bz2-raw, 1=bz2-packbits, 2=lzma-raw, 3=lzma-packbits
    uint32 mask_payload_len
    <mask_payload bytes>
    uint16 spatial_size_bytes  # 2 * 5**3 * 4
    uint16 temporal_size_bytes # 2 * 5**4 * 4
    spatial freqs  : uint16[5,5,5,4]    P(remapped_target | top,left,tl)  frame 0
    temporal freqs : uint16[5,5,5,5,4]  P(remapped_target | top,left,tl,prev) frames 1+
    uint32 bitstream_length
    <bitstream bytes>

Each freq row sums to exactly 2**precision so the decoder infers totals
without storing them. The remap maps the 4 non-peel labels into {0,1,2,3}
in increasing-original-label order (so for peel=2, residual symbols
{0,1,2,3} mean original classes {0,1,3,4}). The context indices on the
spatial/temporal axes are still the full 5-class labels.

Usage:
    python encode_seg_c2split_purepy.py --src seg_targets.bin --peel 2 --roundtrip

    Counts are accumulated only at non-peel positions (the encoder skips

    Last axis is the symbol alphabet. Identical to encode_seg_v3_purepy.quantize_freqs

  1           2 LOAD_CONST               0 ('Build seg_targets.bin via single-class peel split + 4-class AC residual.\n\nDirection 1 (c2 variant) from the parallel-research synthesis 2026-04-11.\nSister module to encode_seg_v3_purepy.py — same range coder, same Markov-3\nspatial / Markov-4 temporal context structure, but the dominant peel class\n(default c2 = sky/background, 49.5% of pixels) is pulled out into a\nseparately compressed binary mask. The arithmetic-coded residual then has\na 4-symbol alphabet over the 50.5% of pixels that aren\'t c2.\n\nCrucial detail: the residual encoder uses the FULL 5-class context (top,\nleft, top-left, prev) — i.e. the model knows when a neighbour was the peel\nclass — even though it only ever EMITS one of the 4 non-peel symbols.\nThat makes it strictly tighter than the c2_entropy.py Strategy B estimate\nwhich collapsed contexts to 4 classes.\n\nOn-disk format ("seg_targets.bin"):\n\n    uint16 n_pairs, uint16 H, uint16 W\n    uint8  precision         # typically 16\n    uint8  peel_class        # 0..N_CLASSES-1\n    uint8  mask_format       # 0=bz2-raw, 1=bz2-packbits, 2=lzma-raw, 3=lzma-packbits\n    uint32 mask_payload_len\n    <mask_payload bytes>\n    uint16 spatial_size_bytes  # 2 * 5**3 * 4\n    uint16 temporal_size_bytes # 2 * 5**4 * 4\n    spatial freqs  : uint16[5,5,5,4]    P(remapped_target | top,left,tl)  frame 0\n    temporal freqs : uint16[5,5,5,5,4]  P(remapped_target | top,left,tl,prev) frames 1+\n    uint32 bitstream_length\n    <bitstream bytes>\n\nEach freq row sums to exactly 2**precision so the decoder infers totals\nwithout storing them. The remap maps the 4 non-peel labels into {0,1,2,3}\nin increasing-original-label order (so for peel=2, residual symbols\n{0,1,2,3} mean original classes {0,1,3,4}). The context indices on the\nspatial/temporal axes are still the full 5-class labels.\n\nUsage:\n    python encode_seg_c2split_purepy.py --src seg_targets.bin --peel 2 --roundtrip\n')
